questions.question: Define an Operating System and explain its role as an intermediary between users and hardware.
keyPoints: OS is system software managing hardware resources
Acts as intermediary between user and computer hardware
Provides environment for convenient program execution
Hides hardware complexity from users

questions.question: Explain the concept of multiprogramming and its advantages in operating systems.
keyPoints: Multiple programs loaded in memory simultaneously
Increases CPU utilization by reducing idle time
When one process waits, CPU executes another
Maximizes throughput of the system

questions.question: What are the different types of operating systems? Explain any two in detail.
keyPoints: Batch OS, Multiprogrammed OS, Time-Sharing OS
Real-Time OS (hard and soft)
Distributed OS and Network OS
Mobile OS and Embedded OS

questions.question: Describe the functions of an Operating System under resource management.
keyPoints: CPU Management via scheduling algorithms
Memory Management including virtual memory
Device Management through drivers
Storage Management via file systems

questions.question: Explain the concept of a process. What are the different states of a process?
keyPoints: Process is a program in execution
Includes code, data, stack, program counter, registers
Process states: New, Ready, Running, Waiting, Terminated
State transitions occur based on events

questions.question: What is the difference between system calls and API? Give examples.
keyPoints: System calls directly interface with kernel
API provides function specifications for applications
System calls are low-level OS services
Examples: read(), write() - system calls; Win32 API, POSIX API

questions.question: Explain the layered structure of an Operating System.
keyPoints: Hardware at lowest layer
Kernel provides basic services
System programs above kernel
User interface at top layer
Each layer uses services of lower layer

questions.question: Discuss in detail the various services provided by an Operating System to users and application programs.
keyPoints: Program execution and termination
I/O operations and file management
Communication between processes
Error detection and reporting
Resource allocation and accounting
Protection and security services
Process synchronization support
Memory management and virtual memory

questions.question: Explain the different types of CPU scheduling algorithms. Compare FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin algorithms.
keyPoints: Long-term, short-term, medium-term scheduling
FCFS: Simple but may cause convoy effect
SJF: Optimal for average waiting time but impossible to implement
Round Robin: Fair time-sharing with quantum
Preemptive vs non-preemptive scheduling
Trade-offs between throughput, waiting time, response time

questions.question: What is virtual memory? Explain the concepts of paging and segmentation in memory management.
keyPoints: Virtual memory extends physical memory using disk
Paging: Fixed-size pages and frames, page table management
Segmentation: Variable-sized segments with logical division
Advantages: Programs larger than memory can run, process isolation
Address translation: Logical to physical address mapping
Page replacement algorithms: FIFO, LRU, Optimal

questions.question: Describe the protection and security mechanisms in modern Operating Systems.
keyPoints: Protection: Controlled access to resources via permissions
Authentication: Passwords, biometrics, tokens
Authorization: Access control lists and capabilities
Encryption: Data protection at rest and in transit
Types of attacks and countermeasures
User and kernel mode separation
